options.text: Configure the basic settings for deploying the Session Host Replacer function app and supporting resources.

elements.name: hostPool
elements.label: Host Pool
elements.toolTip: Select the host pool to automate with Session Host Replacer.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('basics').hostPoolsApi.value, (hp) => parse(concat('{"label":"', hp.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(hp.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"id":"', hp.id, '","name":"', hp.name, '"}}')))]

options.text: The selected host pool must have a valid registration token before deploying session hosts. Without one, the AVD agent cannot register the VMs and they will not appear in the host pool. To generate a new token, go to the host pool in the Azure portal → Overview → Registration key → Generate new key. [Learn more](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-desktop/faq#how-often-should-i-turn-over-the-avd-registration-key)

elements.name: solution
elements.label: Identity Provider
elements.defaultValue: [if(equals(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.vmIdentityType, 'EntraId'), 'Entra Id (Cloud Only Identities) (Uses Storage Keys for FSLogix)', if(contains(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.vmIdentityType, 'ActiveDirectory'), 'Active Directory Domain Services (ADDS)', if(contains(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.vmIdentityType, 'CloudOnly'), 'Entra Kerberos (Cloud Only Identities) (Preview)', if(contains(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.vmIdentityType, 'Kerberos'), 'Entra Kerberos (Hybrid Identities)', 'Entra Domain Services'))))]
elements.toolTip: Choose the identity service provider for your users and session hosts.

allowedValues.label: Entra Id (Cloud Only Identities) (Uses Storage Keys for FSLogix)

elements.name: domainName
elements.label: Domain Name
elements.toolTip: Provide domain name for the selected Virtual Machine Identity solution.

constraints.validationMessage: Provide a valid fully qualified domain name.

elements.name: ouPath
elements.label: OU Path
elements.toolTip: Input the distinguished name of the desired organization unit for the AVD session hosts.

constraints.validationMessage: Provide a valid OU path in Distinguished Name format.

options.text: This add-on requires that the following secret names and values are stored in a key vault that you will select below: 1. VirtualMachineAdminUserName: The virtual machine administrator username and 2. VirtualMachineAdminPassword: The virtual machine administrator password.

This add-on requires that the following secret names and values are stored in a key vault that you will select below: 1. DomainJoinUserPrincipalName: The domain join account UserPrincipalName (i.e., domjoin@contoso.local), 2. DomainJoinUserPassword: The password associated with the domain join user account, 3. VirtualMachineAdminUserName: The virtual machine administrator username, and 4. VirtualMachineAdminPassword: The virtual machine administrator password.

elements.name: keyVault
elements.label: Select Keyvault containing workload secrets
elements.toolTip: Select Keyvault which contains the virtual machine secrets

elements.name: subscription
elements.label: Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the session host VMs. Defaults to the host pool subscription, but can be changed if VMs are in a different subscription.

elements.name: resourceGroup
elements.label: Virtual Machine Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the resource group containing the session host VMs. For greenfield deployments from this solution, resource groups are automatically tagged. For brownfield deployments, any resource group in the selected subscription can be chosen.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('hosts').scope.resourceGroupsApi.value, (rg) => parse(concat('{"label":"', rg.name, '","description":"location: ', rg.location, '","value":{"location":"', rg.location, '","name":"', rg.name, '","id":"', rg.id, '"}}')))]

transforms.list: sort_by(value, &displayName)[?name!='global' && !contains(displayName, '(Stage)')] | [*].{label: displayName, value: { displayName: displayName, name: name }}

elements.name: location
elements.label: Region
elements.toolTip: Select the region where you want to deploy the virtual machines.

elements.name: timeZone
elements.label: Virtual Machines Time Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the time zone for the virtual machines.

allowedValues.label: (UTC+01:00) Amsterdam, Berlin, Bern, Rome, Stockholm, Vienna

elements.name: sessionHostNamePrefix
elements.label: Virtual Machine Name Prefix
elements.toolTip: Provide a Virtual Machine name prefix consisting of alphanumeric and dashes up to 12 characters long. A three character numeric suffix will be appended to the name padded with zeros (0).

validations.message: The prefix can only contain valid character types for computer names.
The length of the virtual machine name prefix and the index padding cannot exceed 15 characters.

elements.name: index
elements.label: Starting number (index)
elements.toolTip: Select the starting index number for the new session hosts. Automatically set to one past the last existing host in the pool.

elements.name: indexPadding
elements.label: Index Padding (number of digits)
elements.toolTip: Select the number of digits used by the index. This value determines the max length of the prefix and also the max number of hosts

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network to which the session hosts will be attached. The list of virtual networks is filtered to those in the same region and subscription selected in the 'Subscription and Region Details' section above.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').network.virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Location: ', vnet.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: subnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the AVD session hosts.

elements.name: enableIPv6
elements.label: Enable IPv6
elements.toolTip: Enables IPv6 support on the network interface. This is an edge case scenario and is not recommended for most deployments. WARNING: Without specific route table entries configured for IPv6 traffic, outbound communication will not work properly.

elements.name: source
elements.label: Image Source
elements.toolTip: Select the type of image to deploy on the session hosts.

elements.name: offer
elements.label: Offer
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image offer.

elements.name: sku
elements.label: SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image SKU.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').image.skuApi, (sku) => and(startsWith(sku.name, 'win'), or(contains(sku.name, 'ent'), contains(sku.name, 'avd')))), (sku) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sku.name, '","value":"', sku.name, '"}')))]

[map(steps('hosts').image.galleriesApi.value, (gal) => parse(concat('{"label":"', gal.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(gal.id, '/'), 4)), ', Location: ', gal.location, '","value":"', gal.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: securityType
elements.label: Security Type
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate Security Type configuration for the Virtual Machine. Note that 'ConfidentialVMs' are not available in all regions and the option may not appear.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(and(not(empty(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.transformed.confidentialVMSizes)), or(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'marketplace'), equals(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'TrustedLaunchAndConfidentialVmSupported'))), parse('[{"label":"ConfidentialVM","value":"ConfidentialVM"},{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"},{"label":"TrustedLaunch","value":"TrustedLaunch"}]'), if(and(not(empty(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.transformed.confidentialVMSizes)), equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'gallery'), equals(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'ConfidentialVM')), parse('[{"label":"ConfidentialVM","value":"ConfidentialVM"}]'), if(and(not(empty(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.transformed.confidentialVMSizes)), equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'gallery'), equals(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'ConfidentialVmSupported')), parse('[{"label":"ConfidentialVM","value":"ConfidentialVM"},{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"}]'), if(and(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'gallery'), equals(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'TrustedLaunch')), parse('[{"label":"TrustedLaunch","value":"TrustedLaunch"}]'), if(or(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'marketplace'), and(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'gallery'), contains(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'TrustedLaunch'), contains(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'Supported'))), parse('[{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"},{"label":"TrustedLaunch","value":"TrustedLaunch"}]'), if(and(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'gallery'), or(equals(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType, 'ConfidentialVmSupported'), empty(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.SecurityType))), parse('[{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"}]'), parse('[]')))))))]

elements.name: secureBootEnabled
elements.label: Enable Secure Boot
elements.toolTip: Secure boot helps protect your VMs against boot kits, rootkits, and kernel-level malware.

elements.name: vTpmEnabled
elements.label: Enable vTPM
elements.toolTip: Virtual Trusted Platform Module (vTPM) is TPM2.0 compliant and validates your VM boot integrity apart from securely storing keys and secrets.

elements.name: integrityMonitoring
elements.label: Integrity Monitoring
elements.toolTip: Integrity monitoring enables cryptographic attestation and verification of VM boot integrity along with monitoring alerts if the VM didn't boot because attestation failed with the defined baseline.

options.text: Encryption at host enhances Azure Disk Storage Server-Side Encryption to ensure that all temp disks and disk caches are encrypted at rest and flow encrypted to the Storage clusters.

elements.name: encryptionAtHost
elements.label: Encryption At Host
elements.toolTip: Check to enable Encryption At Host

options.text: The 'Encryption At Host' feature is not registered in your subscription. Follow the guidance at this link to enable the feature and restart the deployment.

Confidential disk encryption binds disk encryption keys to the virtual machine's TPM and makes the protected disk content accessible only to the VM. The TPM and VM guest state is always encrypted in attested code using keys released by a secure protocol that bypasses the hypervisor and host operating system. Currently only available for the OS disk. Encryption at host may be used for other disks on a Confidential VM in addition to Confidential Disk Encryption.

elements.name: confidentialVMOSDiskEncryption
elements.label: Confidential OS Disk Encryption
elements.toolTip: Check to enable Confidential VM disk encryption

options.text: You can rely on platform-managed keys for the encryption of your managed disk, or you can manage encryption using your own keys. If you choose to manage encryption with your own keys, you must select the existing disk encryption set.

elements.name: selectDiskEncryptionSet
elements.label: Enable Customer-Managed Keys on OS Disk
elements.toolTip: Enables a zero trust configuration on the session host disks.

elements.name: diskEncryptionSetSubscription
elements.label: Disk Encryption Set Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the disk encryption set. For brownfield deployments without tags, you can select any subscription.

elements.name: diskEncryptionSetResourceGroup
elements.label: Disk Encryption Set Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the resource group containing the disk encryption set.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('hosts').diskEncryption.diskEncryptionSetResourceGroupsApi.value, (rg) => parse(concat('{"label":"', rg.name, '","description":"location: ', rg.location, '","value":{"location":"', rg.location, '","name":"', rg.name, '","id":"', rg.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: count
elements.label: Number of Virtual Machines
elements.toolTip: Select the number of session host virtual machines to add to the host pool.

elements.name: diskSku
elements.label: OS Disk SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the disk SKU for the operating system disk.

elements.name: sizeGeneric
elements.label: Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs.

elements.name: sizeConfidentialVM
elements.label: Virtual Machine Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs.

elements.name: enableAcceleratedNetworking
elements.label: Enable accelerated networking
elements.toolTip: Enables low latency and high throughput on the network interface.
elements.visible: [and(bool(first(map(filter(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.value, (sku) => contains(sku.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (sku) => sku.capabilities)), (sku) => equals(sku.name, 'AcceleratedNetworkingEnabled')), (sku) => sku.value))), or(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'marketplace'), bool(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported)))]

elements.name: hibernationEnabled
elements.label: Enable Hibernation
elements.toolTip: Hibernation allows you to pause VMs that aren't being used and save on compute costs where the VMs don't need to run 24/7.
elements.visible: [and(equals(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.type, 'Personal'), bool(first(map(filter(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.value, (sku) => contains(sku.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (sku) => sku.capabilities)), (sku) => equals(sku.name, 'HibernationSupported')), (sku) => sku.value))), or(equals(steps('hosts').image.source, 'marketplace'), bool(steps('hosts').image.imageDefinitionApi.transformed.IsHibernateSupported)))]

elements.name: availability
elements.label: Availability Options
elements.toolTip: Select the redundancy / resiliency for the virtual machines.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(first(map(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.value, (sku) => contains(sku.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (sku) => sku.locationInfo)), (sku) => sku.zones))), parse('[{"label":"No infrastructure redundancy required","value":"None"},{"label":"Availability Sets","value":"AvailabilitySets"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Availability Zones","value":"AvailabilityZones"},{"label":"Availability Sets","value":"AvailabilitySets"},{"label":"No infrastructure redundancy required","value":"None"}]'))]

elements.name: availabilityZones
elements.label: Select Availability Zones
elements.toolTip: Select the desired Availability Zones

constraints.allowedValues: [map(first(map(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').resourceSkusApi.value, (sku) => contains(sku.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (sku) => sku.locationInfo)), (sku) => sku.zones)), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"Zone ', zone, '","value":"', zone, '"}')))]

options.text: Intune Enrollment is not available in Azure Secret or Azure Top Secret cloud environments.

elements.name: intune
elements.label: Intune Enrollment
elements.toolTip: If Intune is configured in your Azure AD tenant, you can choose to have the VM automatically enrolled during the deployment by selecting this checkbox.

options.text: Optionally customize your session host deployment by selecting one or both of the options below. You can customize the AVD Agent and AVD Agent Boot Loader download Urls and/or add custom scripts or software to install during deployment.

elements.name: customizeAgentDownload
elements.label: Customize Agent Download Sources
elements.toolTip: By default, agent installers are downloaded from publicly documented sources (go.microsoft.com links for Internet accessible clouds, aka perma-links for air-gapped cloud environments). Select this checkbox to override the defaults and specify custom URLs or storage account locations for the AVD Agent installers.

elements.name: addCustomScripts
elements.label: Add Custom Scripts or Software
elements.toolTip: Select this checkbox to add custom scripts or software installers to run during session host deployment.

options.text: Artifacts Storage

Optionally select a storage account and blob container hosting your artifacts (agent installers, scripts, or software), and a User Assigned Managed Identity with 'Storage Blob Data Reader' role assignment. If you provide full URLs in the fields below, these selections are not required.

elements.name: storageAccountSubscription
elements.label: Storage Account Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the storage account with your artifacts.

elements.name: storageAccount
elements.label: Artifacts Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account containing your deployment artifacts.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('hosts').customizations.storageAccountsApi.value, (sa) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sa.name, '","description":"Location: ', sa.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(sa.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"id":"', sa.id, '","blobEndpoint":"', sa.properties.primaryEndpoints.blob, '"}}')))]

elements.name: container
elements.label: Artifacts Container
elements.toolTip: Select the blob container which contains your artifact files.

elements.name: managedIdentity
elements.label: Storage Access User Assigned Identity
elements.toolTip: Select a User Assigned Identity with 'Storage Blob Data Reader' role assigned to the storage account selected above.

options.text: AVD Agent Downloads

elements.name: agentBootLoaderUrl
elements.label: [if(empty(steps('hosts').customizations.storageAccount), 'AVD Agent Boot Loader URL', 'AVD Agent Boot Loader URL or Blob Name')]
elements.toolTip: [if(empty(steps('hosts').customizations.storageAccount), 'If left empty, the default behavior is to download from publicly documented sources (go.microsoft.com links for public clouds, aka.ms perma-links for air-gapped clouds). Provide a custom full URL to override the default.', 'If left empty, the default behavior is to download from publicly documented sources (go.microsoft.com links for public clouds, aka.ms perma-links for air-gapped clouds). Provide either a blob name (if using the storage account selected above) or a custom full URL to override the default.')]

constraints.validationMessage: Must be either a full URL (https://...) or a blob name ending in .msi

elements.name: agentUrl
elements.label: [if(empty(steps('hosts').customizations.storageAccount), 'AVD Agent URL', 'AVD Agent URL or Blob Name')]
elements.toolTip: [if(empty(steps('hosts').customizations.storageAccount), 'Optional. Provide a custom full URL to override defaults (go.microsoft.com for public clouds, aka.ms perma-links for air-gapped clouds). This URL will be used as fallback if the endpoint download fails.', 'Optional. Provide a blob name (using storage account above) or full URL to override defaults. This will be used as fallback if the endpoint download fails.')]

constraints.validationMessage: Must be either a full URL (https://...) or a blob name ending in .msi

options.text: Custom Scripts and Software
Specify each customization's name, blob name (or full URI), and optional arguments.

Some software installations may interfere with the host pool join process and should be run after joining completes. Note that the session host will be available for connections before post-join customizations run.

validations.message: The value must not contain spaces or end or begin with a dash or underscore.

elements.name: profileSolution
elements.label: Profile Solution
elements.toolTip: Select the user profile solution for your end users.

elements.name: configureSessionHosts
elements.label: Configure FSLogix registry settings on session hosts

elements.name: fslogixContainerType
elements.label: FSLogix Container Type(s)
elements.toolTip: Select the container type(s) for the FSLogix profiles.

elements.name: sizeInMBs
elements.label: FSLogix VHD Size (MB)
elements.toolTip: Input the max size of the users fslogix containers.

elements.name: storageService
elements.label: Storage Service
elements.toolTip: Select the storage service that provides the SMB file share to support the use of FSLogix.

options.text: Session Host Configuration
Local NetApp Volume
Use the drop down boxes below to select the local NetApp account, capacity pool, and volume that is used for the fslogix profile (and office) containers.

elements.name: existingLocalNetAppAccount
elements.label: Existing Local NetApp Account
elements.toolTip: Select the existing local NetApp account to use for the FSLogix profile storage.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('userProfiles').azureNetAppFiles.netAppAccountsApi.value, (account) => equals(account.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name)), (account) => parse(concat('{"label":"', account.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(account.id, '/'), 4)), '", "value":"', account.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: localCapacityPool
elements.label: Local Capacity Pool
elements.toolTip: Select an existing capacity pool from the selected NetApp Account.

elements.name: localVolumes
elements.label: Local Volume(s)
elements.toolTip: Select the existing volume from the selected NetApp capacity pool.

options.text: Remote Volume
Optionally, use the drop down boxes below to select a remote NetApp account, capacity pool, and volume(s) to use with Cloud Cache profile containers and office containers.

elements.name: remoteNetAppAccount
elements.label: Remote NetApp Account
elements.toolTip: Select the existing remote NetApp account to use for the FSLogix profile storage.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('userProfiles').azureNetAppFiles.netAppAccountsApi.value, (account) => not(equals(account.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name))), (account) => parse(concat('{"label":"', account.name, '","description":"Location: ', account.location, ', Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(account.id, '/'), 4)), '", "value":"', account.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: remoteCapacityPool
elements.label: Remote Capacity Pool
elements.toolTip: Select an existing capacity pool from the selected NetApp Account.

elements.name: remoteVolumes
elements.label: Remote Volume(s)
elements.toolTip: Select the office-containers and profile-containers volumes from the selected NetApp capacity pool.

options.text: Sharding and User Group(s) to Share Assignment(s)

If you chose to Shard the user profile storage, you must provide a group to assign to each storage account. You can choose 'Sharding via Permissions' for simpler configuration at the possible price of longer logon times, or 'Sharding via Object Specific Settings' for more advanced scenarios. You can find instructions on how to configure Object Specific Settings at the link provided.

elements.name: shardingOption
elements.label: Sharding Options
elements.defaultValue: [if(equals(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.fslSharding, 'ShardPerms'), 'Sharding via Permissions', if(equals(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.fslSharding, 'ShardOSS'), 'Sharding via Object Specific Settings', 'No Sharding'))]

constraints.allowedValues: [if(contains(steps('identity').solution, 'EntraKerberos'), parse('[{"label":"No Sharding","value":"None"},{"label":"Sharding via Permissions","value":"ShardPerms"}]'), parse('[{"label":"No Sharding","value":"None"},{"label":"Sharding via Permissions","value":"ShardPerms"},{"label":"Sharding via Object Specific Settings","value":"ShardOSS"}]'))]

informationHeader.informationText: Select the groups to assign to the desktop application group. The group types are automatically filtered based on your identity type.

parameters.searchBoxPlaceHolderText: Enter a string in the group name
parameters.selectedGridNoRowsMessage: You must select at least one group

options.text: The number of groups selected or provided above determines the number of storage accounts that are required when selecting existing accounts.
A total of 1 storage account is required because you chose the 'Entra Id' identity provider.
A total of 1 storage account is required because you have chosen not to Shard your storage.

elements.name: storageCount
elements.label: Number of Existing Storage Accounts Required (Read Only)

options.text: Session Host Configuration
Local Storage Accounts
Select a single storage account from the same region as the session hosts. The session hosts will be configured to store user profiles on this storage account.

Select the same number of storage accounts as the groups selected/provided above from the same region as the session hosts. The session hosts will be configured to store user profiles on these storage accounts.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('userProfiles').azureFiles.storageAccountsApi.transformed.list, (sa) => and(equals(sa.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name), or(and(not(empty(coalesce(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.storageResourceGroup, ''))), equals(first(skip(split(sa.id, '/'), 4)), steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.storageResourceGroup)), empty(coalesce(steps('basics').hostsRGProps.tags.storageResourceGroup, ''))))), (sa) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sa.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(sa.id, '/'), 4)),  ', Location: ', sa.location, '", "value":"', sa.id, '"}')))]

options.text: You have not selected the correct number of storage accounts. You must select the correct number before continuing or the session hosts will not be configured properly.
Remote Storage Accounts

Optionally, select a remote region (not in the same region as your session hosts) and then a single storage account from the selected region for active/active disaster recovery or high availability of user profiles using FSLogix Cloud Cache.

Optionally, select a remote region (not in the same region as your session hosts) and then the same number of storage accounts as the groups selected/provided above in the selected remote region. The session hosts will be configured to also store user profiles on these remote storage accounts using FSLogix Cloud Cache.

elements.name: remoteLocation
elements.label: Remote Location of Storage Account(s)
elements.toolTip: Select the Remote Location.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('userProfiles').azureFiles.storageAccountsApi.transformed.list, (sa) => equals(sa.location, steps('userProfiles').azureFiles.remoteLocation)), (sa) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sa.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(sa.id, '/'), 4)), '", "value":"', sa.id, '"}')))]

options.text: You have not selected the correct number of storage accounts. You must select the correct number before continuing or the session hosts will not be configured properly.

elements.name: enableSessionHostMonitoring
elements.label: Enable Session Host Monitoring (AVD Insights & VM Insights)
elements.toolTip: Deploy the Azure Monitor Agent and configure data collection rules to enable AVD Insights and VM Insights for session hosts.

elements.name: subscription
elements.label: Monitoring Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription where monitoring resources are located

elements.name: avdInsightsDataCollectionRule
elements.label: AVD Insights Data Collection Rule
elements.toolTip: Select the existing AVD Insights Data Collection Rule for session host monitoring.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('monitoring').dataCollectionRulesApi.value, (dcr) => and(contains(dcr.name, 'avdi'), equals(dcr.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name))), (dcr) => parse(concat('{"label":"', dcr.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(dcr.id, '/'), 4)), ', Location: ', dcr.location, '","value":"', dcr.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: vmInsightsDataCollectionRule
elements.label: VM Insights Data Collection Rule
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Virtual Machines Insights Data Collection Rule.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('monitoring').dataCollectionRulesApi.value, (dcr) => and(contains(dcr.name, 'MSVMI'), equals(dcr.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name))), (dcr) => parse(concat('{"label":"', dcr.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(dcr.id, '/'), 4)), ', Location: ', dcr.location, '","value":"', dcr.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: dataCollectionEndpoint
elements.label: Data Collection Endpoint
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Data Collection Endpoint for session host monitoring.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('monitoring').dataCollectionEndpointsApi.value, (dce) => equals(dce.location, steps('hosts').scope.location.name)), (dce) => parse(concat('{"label":"', dce.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(dce.id, '/'), 4)), ', Location: ', dce.location, '","value":"', dce.id, '"}')))]

options.text: This step is only needed for brownfield deployments where your host pool uses non-standard naming conventions. Leave 'Use Custom Naming' unchecked to automatically derive names from your host pool naming pattern.

elements.name: enableCustomNaming
elements.label: Use Custom Naming Overrides
elements.toolTip: Check this box to explicitly define resource names. Required for brownfield deployments with non-standard host pool naming (e.g., 'prod-avd-hostpool-01' instead of 'hp-avd-prod-eus'). If unchecked, names are automatically derived from the host pool name.

options.text: CRITICAL: Session host naming conventions MUST match your existing VMs in the host pool! New VMs deployed as replacements will use these patterns. Use 'SHNAME' token for session host name and '##' token for availability set index.

Examples:
• If existing VMs are 'avdvm-001', use 'SHNAME' (no prefix/suffix)
• If existing VMs are 'vm-avdvm-001', use 'vm-SHNAME'
• If existing VMs are 'avdvm-001-vm', use 'SHNAME-vm'

elements.name: virtualMachineNameConvOverride
elements.label: Virtual Machine Naming Convention
elements.toolTip: Required. Naming pattern for session host VMs. Use SHNAME token which will be replaced with the session host name (e.g., 'vm-SHNAME' becomes 'vm-avdvm-001'). This MUST match your existing VM naming.

constraints.validationMessage: VM naming convention must include the SHNAME token.

elements.name: diskNameConvOverride
elements.label: OS Disk Naming Convention
elements.toolTip: Required. Naming pattern for OS disks. Use SHNAME token (e.g., 'disk-SHNAME' becomes 'disk-avdvm-001'). Should align with existing disk naming.

constraints.validationMessage: Disk naming convention must include the SHNAME token.

elements.name: networkInterfaceNameConvOverride
elements.label: Network Interface Naming Convention
elements.toolTip: Required. Naming pattern for NICs. Use SHNAME token (e.g., 'nic-SHNAME' becomes 'nic-avdvm-001'). Should align with existing NIC naming.

constraints.validationMessage: NIC naming convention must include the SHNAME token.

elements.name: availabilitySetNameConvOverride
elements.label: Availability Set Naming Convention
elements.toolTip: Required. Naming pattern for availability sets. Use ## token for index (e.g., 'avset-##' becomes 'avset-01', 'avset-02'). Only used if availability is set to 'AvailabilitySets'.

constraints.validationMessage: Availability set naming convention must include the ## token for indexing.
